item.title: Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy Exon Skipping
item.description:
• Summary: A breakthrough in exon skipping therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) is highlighted, focusing on a mother's advocacy. Exon skipping approaches, such as antisense oligonucleotides like eteplirsen, aim to skip specific exons in the dystrophin gene to produce functional protein and slow disease progression. The article discusses recent advancements and patient-centric perspectives.
  • MoAs: Exon skipping drugs work by binding to pre-mRNA to promote exclusion of mutated exons, allowing production of partially functional dystrophin protein.
  • Sources: STAT News
